title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))

       country continent  year  life_expectancy  population_millions  \
0  Afghanistan      Asia  1952           28.801             8.425333   
1  Afghanistan      Asia  1957           30.332             9.240934   
2  Afghanistan      Asia  1962           31.997            10.267083   

   gdp_per_capita

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page explores the relationship between life expectancy and GDP per capita using the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")
df.head()

latest_year = df["year"].max()
latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(latest["gdp_per_capita"], latest["life_expectancy"], alpha=0.7)
plt.xscale("log")
plt.title(f"Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita ({latest_year})")
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy (years)")
plt.show()

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page shows how life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population change over time for one country in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

country_name = "India"  # change this to any country in the dataset
c = df[df["country"] == country_name].sort_values("year")
c[["year", "life_expectancy", "gdp_per_capita", "population_millions"]]

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["life_expectancy"], marker="o")
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Life Expectancy Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy (years)")
plt.show()

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["gdp_per_capita"], marker="o")
plt.title(f"{country_name}: GDP per Capita Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.show()

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["population_millions"], marker="o")
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Population Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Population (millions)")
plt.show()

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page shows how life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population change over time for one country in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

country_name = "India"  # change this to any country in the dataset
c = df[df["country"] == country_name].sort_values("year")
c[["year", "life_expectancy", "gdp_per_capita", "population_millions"]]

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["life_expectancy"], marker="o")
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Life Expectancy Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy (years)")
plt.show()

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["gdp_per_capita"], marker="o")
plt.title(f"{country_name}: GDP per Capita Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.show()

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["population_millions"], marker="o")
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Population Over Time")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Population (millions)")
plt.show()

title: Gapminder Website
text: The Gapminder dataset contains life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population data for 142 countries from 1952 to 2007 in 5-year intervals.

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
This page explores the relationship between life expectancy and population using the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")
df.head()

latest_year = df["year"].max()
latest = df[df["year"] == latest_year].copy()

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(latest["population_millions"], latest["life_expectancy"], alpha=0.7)
plt.title(f"Life Expectancy vs Population ({latest_year})")
plt.xlabel("Population (millions)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy (years)")
plt.show()

corr = latest["population_millions"].corr(latest["life_expectancy"])
corr








0.04755311503282725
